title: feat: restore bun demo runtime for latest-code cluster loop
motivation: Contributors currently cannot run a single bun demo command that provisions a cluster and runs the latest local code in-cluster. The existing docs/history imply this capability, but runtime behavior is incomplete, causing onboarding friction and incorrect expectations.
behaviour: Add a bun demo workflow that provisions or reuses a local k3d cluster, deploys application runtime from current local sources, waits for readiness/ingress reachability, and supports an interactive watch-mode rollout loop so file changes can be propagated to the cluster runtime without manual ad-hoc steps.

in: Add a new bun demo command entry point wired in package scripts.
Implement local demo orchestration script for cluster bootstrap, migration, image build/import, manifest apply, and readiness checks.
Provide interactive watch-mode rollout actions that can refresh deployed runtime after source changes.
Document or align local demo lifecycle behavior to the implemented script semantics.
out: Production deploy pipeline changes or remote environment rollout policy changes.
Introduction of new cloud infrastructure components.
Refactoring unrelated runtime subsystems not required for bun demo orchestration.

acceptance_criteria: `bun run demo` exists and launches a local Kubernetes demo flow end-to-end on a clean machine with prerequisites installed.
The demo flow deploys app runtime built from current local code (not stale remote image tags) and exposes a reachable local URL.
Readiness checks and failure messages are explicit for cluster/bootstrap/deploy phases.
Watch-mode prompts allow a developer to trigger rollout actions after file changes without manually re-running all setup steps.
test_plan: Run `.agents/scripts/feature/validate-issue-json.sh` for the generated issue payload and ensure it passes.
Execute `bun run demo` once on a workstation with docker+k3d+kubectl+bun and confirm cluster provisioning, image deployment, and URL readiness.
Modify a file under watched paths and verify rollout action updates the running deployment successfully.
Run `bun run demo --status` and `bun run demo --delete` to validate lifecycle helper actions.
